_comment: TEMPLATE. Copy this file to `config/qwen.local.json` (gitignored) and fill in real values. At load time the server DEEP-MERGES qwen.local.json OVER qwen.json, so put ALL secrets, your GCP project id, credential paths, exact model ids, and `enabled` flags here — never in the committed qwen.json. Delete keys you don't override.

openai._note: GPT/Codex. Export OPENAI_API_KEY (never put keys in config), set the model, flip enabled.

groq_example._note: EXAMPLE of a config-only custom provider — any OpenAI-compatible endpoint works (Groq, OpenRouter, Mistral, LM Studio http://127.0.0.1:1234/v1, vLLM...). Rename freely; then delegate(provider="groq_example").

gemini._location_note: Region where the models are served. NEWER/PREVIEW models (e.g. Gemini 3.x) are ONLY on the 'global' endpoint — use "global". Older GA models (2.5) also work in regional endpoints like us-central1. If a call returns 404 NOT_FOUND ('model not found ... in the specified region'), switch this to "global".

models._note: BARE Vertex model ids (google-genai SDK, used by the `delegate` tool). Examples below; use the exact ids your project can access.

cost._note: YOUR real Vertex prices in USD per MILLION tokens (check the current price page). Enables est_cost_usd metering + the usd_per_day budget below.

models._note: litellm ids (used by the `assign` tool / Aider). MUST use the `vertex_ai/` prefix for a service account — NOT `gemini/`. The server injects VERTEXAI_LOCATION from providers.gemini.location above (use 'global' for Gemini 3.x).

budgets._note: ENFORCED daily ceilings so a runaway retry loop can't drain cloud credits. 0 = no cap. Token caps always work; usd caps need the provider's `cost` prices set.
